title: cassia javanica subsp. microcalyx
intro: Cassia javanica subsp. microcalyx, also known as the small-flowered Senna, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Cassia javanica subsp. microcalyx include treating digestive issues such as constipation, diarrhea, and dysentery, as well as reducing fever and alleviating menstrual cramps. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of various conditions.
